div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, Greeting: Know that I have granted to the abbot and convent of Holyrood that no-one may take poinds from them or from their men unless for their own debts or for the debts of their men. Wherefore I firmly forbid that anyone either in Scotia, or on the Isle of May when the men of the aforesaid abbot come there to fish, may take poinds from the canons of Holyrood or from their men unless it be for their demesne debts, or for the debts of their men, on pain of our forfeiture. As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Richard de Prebenda my clerk. At Haddington.
